Patch Set 4:

> A client will try to glimpse file size when the file is being restored, and then stuck on layout lock.

It won't "get stuck." It will wait for it to be released at the end of restore. What's wrong with that?

Patch Set 4:

Without that patch, a command like 'stat' will block until the restore is complete, and I do see stack traces in the kernel, so it's not pretty.
With this patch, under the same conditions, 'stat' returns instantly.

I would +1 this patch; it just works for me, but I don't know whether it's correct nor bug free.

Patch Set 4:

"It won't "get stuck." It will wait for it to be released at the end of restore. What's wrong with that"

What's wrong with that??? It could take very long time to restore a file. Your attitude here is wasting everybody's time.

Abandoned

I'm killing this patch since John dislikes it.

Patch Set 4:

> Abandoned
 > 
 > I'm killing this patch since John dislikes it.

Why? This patch solves a problem. One day one of our customer is going to ask why an `ls` is getting stuck while their 10TB file is getting moved. I tested this patch and it works as designed. Unless there is a technical reason not to have it, it should be resurrected (again!)

Patch Set 4:

Frank,

I don't like MS_RESTORE/LLIF_FILE_RESTORING and was hoping to remove them. See https://jira.hpdd.intel.com/browse/LU-6460.

I also don't like it when we take and immediately release locks to make something correct. These flags seem like a poor fix to me. But I understand the motivation behind this change and wouldn't hold it up.

Patch Set 4:

Thanks John. I worry that a tool like robinhood could get stuck while stating files. If this patch is not good enough, would a patch for 6460 also fix this one?

(1 comment)

Jinshan will you un-abandon this patch?

Patch Set 5: Code-Review-1

(1 comment)

Keeping a reference on the object during restore sets us up for double find hangs. If the object is removed during restore then we'll hang in hsm_cdt_request_completed(). I tried to reproduce this bt found an earlier hang when the CT tries to get the path of the FID being restored.

# lfs hsm_archive f0
# lfs hsm_state f0
f0: (0x00000009) exists archived, archive_id:1
# lfs hsm_release f0
# lfs hsm_state f0
f0: (0x0000000d) released exists archived, archive_id:1

parents.subject: LU-4840 lfs: Use file lease to implement migration

commit.subject: LU-4727 hsm: flush UPDATE lock for restore
commit.message:
LU-4727 hsm: flush UPDATE lock for restore

When a file is being restored, the MDT has to hold its layout lock.
The MDT uses MOF_RESTORE object flag to mark files in restore state to
prevent glimpse from waiting for the restore to complete.

If object flag MOF_RESTORE on MDT object is set, the MS_RESTORE flag
is transferred back to client by GETATTR request. To make sure that
the flag is surely seen by copy tool client, the MDT has to flush
UPDATE lock before acquiring LAYOUT lock.

Signed-off-by: Jinshan Xiong <jinshan.xiong@intel.com>
Change-Id: I8d11d77a52e2ca35088ab0437b55fcadbd3f5057
